title: Oil and Water dont mix
wikitext:
[]: Oil and Water don't mix.             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jul 10, 2008 10:30 pm But what about blood and LCL? In EoE, when Asuka/Unit-02 gets impaled in the eye by the LoL copy, blood immediately starts gushing out of her eye. But it acts as if it's in air. But it's in another liquid, the blood of Lilith/LCL. Now, does that mean LCL is almost thin as air, or Asuka's blood is thicker than it should be, or did somebody fuck up?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 10, 2008 10:45 pm Same thing happens with Rei's tears in ep23. It's dissatisfying, by my bet is on willful ignorance of fluid dynamics on the part of the staff for the purposes of making animation easier.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Jul 10, 2008 10:46 pm I'd go with 'fuck up' on this. They did the same thing when Rei cried in Unit 00 in episode 23. ^ You bastard! Beat me by a couple of minutes!

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 10, 2008 10:50 pm ^ I aim to please!

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Fri Jul 11, 2008 7:44 am Oh come on...you really expect them to have sort of a growing cloud of blood in front of Asuka's eye? You really expect them to have Rei's tears just kind of floating off? That would be WAY more WTF than the technical inaccuracies, and it would totally detract from the scene as people are trying to figure out what's going on.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 13, 2008 5:23 pm How's Rei supposed to gape at her tears if they instantly dissipate into the LCL? ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jul 13, 2008 6:58 pm http://www.evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=3778

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Jul 13, 2008 9:45 pm It's done for dramatic effect.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Mon Jul 14, 2008 3:47 am Actually you do see Shinji's blood suspending in the LCL in the rebuild movie.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jul 14, 2008 8:14 am tinmeigut wrote: Actually you do see Shinji's blood suspending in the LCL in the rebuild movie.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Mon Jul 14, 2008 8:42 am After Eva 01 was forcefully 'sunk' back underground at the first encounter with Ramiel (designated as 6th Angel in the Rebuild), there was a shot which showed that CPR was given to Shinji by the plgusuit. That shot also showed that Shinji had possibly had nosebleeding, as a gush of blood was seen to be suspending in the LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by p06 » Mon Jul 14, 2008 8:49 am screwup sounds the most likely tempting to fanwank; but probably has been raised before: Those scenes don't really portray the actual physical view of the entry plug, but shows some sort of the view from the pilots' souls. Like when they're connected to the evas they go into some dreamlike trance (realize how they can see outside of the evas when the entry plug is like enclosed, this seems to suggests a "brain in jar" sort of thing where what they experience is different from what is physically happening) so technically their souls are crying/bleeding/etc. in that trance but not physicall?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jul 14, 2008 8:52 am Good eye. Maybe they'll fix this 'LCL physics' crap in Rebuild. Or just confuse the whole subject even more. My guess is the latter.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Mon Jul 14, 2008 8:55 am p06 wrote: screwup sounds the most likely Those scenes don't really portray the actual physical view of the entry plug, but shows some sort of the view from the pilots' souls. Like when they're connected to the evas they go into some dreamlike trance (realize how they can see outside of the evas when the entry plug is like enclosed, this seems to suggests a "brain in jar" sort of thing where what they experience is different from what is physically happening) so technically their souls are crying/bleeding/etc. in that trance but not physicall?

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Jul 14, 2008 10:07 am p06 wrote: Those scenes don't really portray the actual physical view of the entry plug, but shows some sort of the view from the pilots' souls. Like when they're connected to the evas they go into some dreamlike trance (realize how they can see outside of the evas when the entry plug is like enclosed, this seems to suggests a "brain in jar" sort of thing where what they experience is different from what is physically happening) so technically their souls are crying/bleeding/etc. in that trance but not physicall?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 14, 2008 12:19 pm I think we're pretty much seeing what the pilots are seeing. The entry plug uses screen displays to convey the Eva's surroundings to the pilot ("screens" is written on the actual production art). The behavioral logic of LCL in the plug is most easily chalked up to artistic liberties. As for Rebuild, it's after Shinji is recovered from Ramiel's attack and his nose is bleeding.

[]:             Quote Post by Rhyno » Mon Jul 14, 2008 11:22 pm If you haven't read the thread provided by Ornette, I suggest you do so. I know that it is seven pages long, but it is worth the read. RahOtaku tries to turn it into a religious discussion, but there is something to be said for Dr. Nick's LCL liquid/gas theory.

[]:             Quote Post by p06 » Tue Jul 15, 2008 9:29 am tinmeigut wrote: You raised an interesting argument which I had never thought about before. Sorry to be a bit lazy but I don't want to dig into the piles of old topics in order to find evidence to support your saying; can you suggest some to support your ground? Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jul 15, 2008 10:03 am Solution: Asuka bleeds oil.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 15, 2008 1:42 pm p06 wrote: seem to suggest it highly likely that their souls are interacting with the evas' directly.
